headline: Broadcom, Target Earnings and Jobs Report: What to Watch This Week
summary: A heavy slate of economic data headlines the first week of March, culminating in the February jobs report. Investors will also weigh earnings from companies including Broadcom and Target, as well as market fallout from the weekend strikes on Iran.

content.title: Broadcom, Target Earnings and Jobs Report: What to Watch This Week
content.summary: A heavy slate of economic data headlines the first week of March, culminating in the February jobs report. Investors will also weigh earnings from companies including Broadcom and Target, as well as market fallout from the weekend strikes on Iran.

headline: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Financial Results
summary: MIAMI, March 02, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. (NYSE: NCLH) (together with NCL Corporation Ltd. (“NCLC”), “Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings”, “Norwegian”, “NCLH” or the “Company”) today reported financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2025, and provided guidance for the first quarter and full year 2026. Highlights Grew total revenue 3.7% to $9.8 billion in 2025. GAAP net income was $423.2 million, with EPS of $0.92.Delivered Adjusted

content.title: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Financial Results
content.summary: MIAMI, March 02, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. (NYSE: NCLH) (together with NCL Corporation Ltd. (“NCLC”), “Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings”, “Norwegian”, “NCLH” or the “Company”) today reported financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2025, and provided guidance for the first quarter and full year 2026. Highlights Grew total revenue 3.7% to $9.8 billion in 2025. GAAP net income was $423.2 million, with EPS of $0.92.Delivered Adjusted

headline: Norwegian Cruise Line falls on weak guidance despite Q4 beat
summary: Investing.com -- Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. (NYSE:NCLH) reported fourth quarter results that beat earnings expectations but missed on revenue, while issuing fiscal 2026 guidance that fell short of analyst estimates, sending shares down 7.8% in premarket trading.

content.title: Norwegian Cruise Line falls on weak guidance despite Q4 beat
content.summary: Investing.com -- Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. (NYSE:NCLH) reported fourth quarter results that beat earnings expectations but missed on revenue, while issuing fiscal 2026 guidance that fell short of analyst estimates, sending shares down 7.8% in premarket trading.

headline: Norwegian Cruise Line (NYSE:NCLH) Reports Sales Below Analyst Estimates In Q4 CY2025 Earnings, Stock Drops
summary: Cruise company Norwegian Cruise Line (NYSE:NCLH) fell short of the market’s revenue expectations in Q4 CY2025, but sales rose 6.4% year on year to $2.24 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.28 per share was 5.5% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: Norwegian Cruise Line (NYSE:NCLH) Reports Sales Below Analyst Estimates In Q4 CY2025 Earnings, Stock Drops
content.summary: Cruise company Norwegian Cruise Line (NYSE:NCLH) fell short of the market’s revenue expectations in Q4 CY2025, but sales rose 6.4% year on year to $2.24 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.28 per share was 5.5% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Norwegian Cruise Line: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
summary: The average estimate of seven analysts surveyed by Zacks Investment Research was also for earnings of 28 cents per share. The cruise operator posted revenue of $2.24 billion in the period, which missed Street forecasts. Seven analysts surveyed by Zacks expected $2.35 billion.

content.title: Norwegian Cruise Line: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
content.summary: The average estimate of seven analysts surveyed by Zacks Investment Research was also for earnings of 28 cents per share. The cruise operator posted revenue of $2.24 billion in the period, which missed Street forecasts. Seven analysts surveyed by Zacks expected $2.35 billion.

headline: Cruise Stocks Sink as Middle East Conflict Interrupts Shipping
summary: Shares of cruise operators fell as investors anticipate higher fuel costs and shipping interruptions resulting from the escalating Middle East conflict.  Shares of Royal Caribbean (RCL) fell 3% to around $301.

content.title: Cruise Stocks Sink as Middle East Conflict Interrupts Shipping
content.summary: Shares of cruise operators fell as investors anticipate higher fuel costs and shipping interruptions resulting from the escalating Middle East conflict.  Shares of Royal Caribbean (RCL) fell 3% to around $301.

headline: Norwegian Cruise Line Stock Tumbles After Earnings Beat. Iran Eclipses Its Numbers.
summary: FEATURE  What a day to report earnings. Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings sailed ahead with the release of its fourth-quarter results ahead of the open Monday. But its earnings beat will be completely overshadowed by the impact of the escalating conflict in the Middle East.

headline: Dow Jones leads modest retreat as Wall Street aloof to Iran war
summary: 10am: Wall Street starts with modest falls Initial trading in New York was less pessimistic than in Europe, with losses for the main US indices considerably smaller in initial trades. The Dow Jones is the worst, down 0.65%, followed by the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq Composite both falling...

content.title: Dow Jones leads modest retreat as Wall Street aloof to Iran war
content.summary: 10am: Wall Street starts with modest falls Initial trading in New York was less pessimistic than in Europe, with losses for the main US indices considerably smaller in initial trades. The Dow Jones is the worst, down 0.65%, followed by the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq Composite both falling...
